rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM H BRADLEY VS. DOLMEN PROPERTY GROUP LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov 9, 2018 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2nd AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT sustained without leave to amend. If Cross-Complainant wishes to contest the tentative ruling and seek a leave to amend, Cross-Complainant is to provide a list of proposed factual allegations re: delayed discovery in light of current allegations (SACC 7:21-23) and Santillian v. Roma Catholic Bishop of Fresno (2008) 163 Cal. App. 4th 4. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY UNOBSKEY VS. SIMON MICHAEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendants Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Simon Michaels First Amended Cross Complaint And Motion For Attorneys Fees Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov 9, 2018 line 3. PLAINTIFF SIDNEY UNOBSKEY's Special Motion To Strike Simon Michaels First Amended Cross Complaint And Motion For Attorneys Fees Ccp 425.16 DENIED. "At the first step [of ANTI-SLAPP analysis], the moving defendant bears the burden of identifying all allegations of protected activity, and the claims for relief supported by them." Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. Moving party moves to strike the First Amended Cross-Complaint in its entirety. (See Notice of Motion). As to the claims for negligence and deprivation of support, there are no protected activities (even arguably protected activities) that are alleged in support of these claims (paragraphs incorporated by reference do not support these claims for relief). As to other claims in the Frist Amended Cross-Complaint, the moving party was provided an opportunity to identify allegations of protected activity. Moving party failed to do so. For example, moving party claims that paragraph 69 of the FAXC is protected activity. ("In 2013, Michael, as part of his foundation work, dug piers on his property line adjacent to SIDNEY and NANCY's property.") No argument and no authority is provided to support a claim that this allegation (or other similar allegations) pertain to protected activity. To the extent that the moving party asserts that activities listed as protected (e.g. paragraph 110(d)) have been engaged into by Millar and Sabin, moving party failed to show that any claims against these cross-defendants arise out of the letters, appeals, or testimony, as opposed to statements made directly to Michael or his agents or non-protected underlying conduct. Additionally, Unobskeys' motion is untimely. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARIE TATMAN VS. ELIJAH WHITE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Defendant Carrol Anne Whites Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov 9, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANT CARROL WHITE's Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication continued to November 21, 2018. Even though opposition was filed on October 26, 2018, an incomplete courtesy copy of opposition has been delivered to department 501 after 4:30 p.m. on November 7, 2018 in violation of SFLR 2.7B and without any explanation for the delay; and a purportedly complete copy was delivered at 9:20 a.m. on November 6, 2018. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MEI HAR YEE VS. HELEN MANDEVILLE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov 9, 2018 line 8. DEFENDANT HELEN MANDEVILLE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege whether (1) place of business can not be ascertained; or (2) or a person of suitable age or discretion there can not be found. Proof of Service, on which Plaintiff relies, contains both of these alternative (and factually inconsistent) options. = (501/CFH)
